turns.speaker: Dr Hughes
turns.text: Come in, Tom, Sara. So, how's the project on cycling to campus coming along? Have you settled on your methods yet?

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.text: We think so. We're planning to combine an online survey with a set of interviews.

turns.speaker: Sara
turns.text: Right. The survey gives us the numbers — how many people cycle, how far, and so on — and the interviews let us dig into why people choose to cycle, or why they don't.

turns.speaker: Dr Hughes
turns.text: Good. A mixed-methods approach makes a lot of sense here. Now, have you thought carefully about your sample? A very common mistake is to only survey people who already cycle.

turns.speaker: Sara
turns.text: Yes, we talked about exactly that. If we only ask cyclists, we'll miss the whole point — we really need to hear from people who've given up cycling, or who've never started.

turns.speaker: Dr Hughes
turns.text: Precisely. So make sure you recruit widely.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.text: We were actually going to hand out flyers at the bike racks, but you're right, that would completely bias the sample. So we'll advertise through the whole student email list instead.

turns.speaker: Dr Hughes
turns.text: Much better. That way you reach cyclists and non-cyclists alike. Now, let's divide up the work. Who's going to do what?

turns.speaker: Sara
turns.text: Well, I'll design the survey questions — that's honestly the part I enjoy most.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.text: And I'll set up and run the interviews, and do the transcribing afterwards. I've done interviewing before, so it makes sense for me to take that on.

turns.speaker: Sara
turns.text: The one thing is the statistics. Neither of us is very confident with the numbers side of it.

turns.speaker: Dr Hughes
turns.text: That's absolutely fine — I'll run the statistical analysis together with you in our weekly sessions, so leave that part to me for now. You two focus on collecting good data.

turns.speaker: Tom
turns.text: That's a relief. Thanks, Dr Hughes.

questionGroups.instructions_html: According to Sara, what is the main advantage of adding interviews?

options.text: They reach a larger number of people.
They reveal people's reasons for cycling or not.
They take less time than a survey.
